sentences.text: Era la víspera del Domingo de Ramos
sentences.original: I THE evening service was being celebrated on the eve of Palm Sunday

sentences.text: y en el viejo convento de Petrovsky

sentences.original: in the Old Petrovsky Convent.

sentences.text: Cuando empezaron a repartir las ramas de palma,
sentences.original: When they began distributing the palm

sentences.text: eran casi las diez de la noche.
sentences.original: it was close upon ten o'clock,

sentences.text: Las velas ardían con poca luz
sentences.original: the candles were burning dimly,

sentences.text: y las mechas necesitaban ser cortadas.
sentences.original: the wicks wanted snuffing;

sentences.text: Todo estaba envuelto en una especie de neblina.
sentences.original: it was all in a sort of mist.

sentences.text: En la penumbra de la iglesia,
sentences.original: In the twilight of the church

sentences.text: la multitud parecía moverse como el mar.
sentences.original: the crowd seemed heaving like the sea,
and to Bishop Pyotr,

sentences.translationRu: уже три дня чувствовал себя нехорошо.
sentences.original: who had been unwell for the last three days,

sentences.text: Le parecía que todos los rostros
sentences.original: it seemed that all the faces--old and young, men's and women's--were alike,

sentences.text: —viejos y jóvenes, de hombres y de mujeres—
sentences.translationRu: старые и молодые, мужские и женские —

sentences.text: Todos los que se acercaban a recibir la palma
sentences.translationRu: Все, кто подходил за ветвью пальмы,
sentences.original: that everyone who came up for the palm

sentences.text: tenían la misma expresión en los ojos.
sentences.original: had the same expression in his eyes.

sentences.text: En la neblina no podía ver las puertas,
sentences.translationRu: В тумане он не мог видеть дверей,
sentences.original: In the mist he could not see the doors;
the crowd kept moving
and looked as though there were no end to it.
The female choir was singing,

sentences.text: y una monja leía las oraciones del día.
sentences.original: a nun was reading the prayers for the day.

sentences.text: ¡Qué calor tan sofocante había en la iglesia!
sentences.translationRu: Какая удушающая жара царила в церкви!
sentences.original: How stifling, how hot it was!
How long the service went on!
Bishop Pyotr was tired.
His breathing was laboured
and rapid,
his throat was parched,

sentences.text: y los hombros le dolían de cansancio.
sentences.original: his shoulders ached with weariness,
his legs were trembling.
And it disturbed him unpleasantly when a religious maniac uttered occasional shrieks

sentences.text: que a veces lanzaba un fanático religioso desde la galería.
sentences.translationRu: которые время от времени издавал взволнованный человек с галереи.
sentences.original: in the gallery.

sentences.text: De repente, como en un sueño o en un delirio,
sentences.translationRu: Вдруг, словно во сне или в бреду,
sentences.original: And then all of a sudden, as though in a dream or delirium,

sentences.text: el obispo creyó ver a su madre, Marya Timofyevna.
sentences.translationRu: епископ решил, что видит свою мать, Марью Тимофеевну.
sentences.original: it seemed to the bishop as though his own mother Marya Timofyevna,

sentences.text: Hacía nueve años que no la veía.
sentences.translationRu: Он не видел её девять лет.
sentences.original: whom he had not seen for nine years,

sentences.text: O tal vez era una anciana muy parecida a ella.
sentences.translationRu: А может быть, это была пожилая женщина, очень похожая на неё.
sentences.original: or some old woman just like his mother,

sentences.text: La mujer salió de la multitud,
sentences.original: came up to him out of the crowd, and,

sentences.original: after taking a palm branch from him,
walked away

sentences.text: Sonreía con una sonrisa amable y alegre
sentences.original: looking at him all the while good-humouredly with a kind, joyful smile

sentences.text: hasta que desapareció entre la gente.
sentences.original: until she was lost in the crowd.
And for some reason

sentences.text: las lágrimas cayeron por el rostro del obispo.
sentences.original: tears flowed down his face.
There was peace in his heart,
everything was well,

sentences.text: Sin embargo, no podía dejar de mirar hacia el coro de la izquierda,
sentences.translationRu: Тем не менее он не мог оторвать взгляда от левого хора,
sentences.original: yet he kept gazing fixedly towards the left choir,
where the prayers were being read,

sentences.text: En la penumbra de la tarde
sentences.original: where in the dusk of evening

sentences.text: no se podía reconocer a nadie allí.
sentences.original: you could not recognize anyone,
and--wept.

sentences.text: Las lágrimas brillaban en su rostro y en su barba.
sentences.translationRu: Слёзы блестели на его лице и в бороде.
sentences.original: Tears glistened on his face and on his beard.

sentences.text: Cerca de él alguien también empezó a llorar,
sentences.translationRu: Рядом с ним кто-то тоже начал плакать,
sentences.original: Here someone close at hand was weeping,
then someone else farther away,
then others and still others,
and little by little

sentences.text: la iglesia se llenó de un llanto suave.
sentences.original: the church was filled with soft weeping.
And a little later, within five minutes,

sentences.text: el coro de las monjas volvió a cantar.
sentences.original: the nuns' choir was singing;
no one was weeping
and everything was as before.
Soon the service was over.

sentences.text: Cuando el obispo subió a su carruaje para volver a casa,
sentences.translationRu: Когда епископ сел в экипаж, чтобы ехать домой,
sentences.original: When the bishop got into his carriage to drive home,

sentences.text: el alegre repique de las campanas pesadas y costosas
sentences.translationRu: весёлый мелодичный звон тяжёлых дорогих колоколов
sentences.original: the gay, melodious chime of the heavy, costly bells

sentences.text: llenaba todo el jardín a la luz de la luna.
sentences.translationRu: заполнял весь сад при лунном свете.
sentences.original: was filling the whole garden in the moonlight.
The white walls,

sentences.text: las cruces blancas sobre las tumbas,
sentences.original: the white crosses on the tombs,

sentences.text: los abedules blancos y las sombras negras,
sentences.original: the white birch-trees and black shadows,

sentences.text: y la luna lejana en el cielo justo encima del convento
sentences.translationRu: и далёкая луна в небе прямо над монастырём,
sentences.original: and the far-away moon in the sky exactly over the convent,

sentences.text: Era una vida apartada e incomprensible,
sentences.translationRu: Это была жизнь отдельная и непостижимая,
sentences.original: seemed now living their own life, apart and incomprehensible,
yet very near to man.
It was the beginning of April,

sentences.text: y después del cálido día de primavera
sentences.original: and after the warm spring day

sentences.text: la noche se había vuelto fresca.
sentences.original: it turned cool;
there was a faint touch of frost,

sentences.text: y en el aire frío y suave
sentences.original: and the breath of spring could be felt in the soft, chilly air.

sentences.text: se sentía el aliento de la primavera.
El camino desde el convento hasta la ciudad era arenoso
sentences.translationRu: Дорога от монастыря до города была песчаной,
sentences.original: The road from the convent to the town was sandy,

sentences.text: y los caballos tenían que ir al paso.
sentences.original: the horses had to go at a walking pace,
and on both sides of the carriage

sentences.text: en la brillante y tranquila luz de la luna,
sentences.original: in the brilliant, peaceful moonlight

sentences.text: la gente caminaba por la arena de vuelta a casa desde la iglesia.
sentences.translationRu: люди шли по песку домой из церкви.
sentences.original: there were people trudging along home from church through the sand.
And all was silent,
sunk in thought;

sentences.text: Todo lo de alrededor parecía amable, joven y familiar:
sentences.translationRu: Всё вокруг казалось добрым, молодым и родным:
sentences.original: everything around seemed kindly, youthful, akin,

sentences.text: los árboles, el cielo e incluso la luna.
sentences.original: everything--trees and sky and even the moon,
and one longed to think
that so it would be always.

sentences.text: Por fin el carruaje entró en la ciudad
sentences.original: At last the carriage drove into the town

sentences.text: y avanzó con ruido por la calle principal.
sentences.translationRu: и с грохотом покатил по главной улице.
sentences.original: and rumbled along the principal street.
The shops were already shut,

sentences.text: pero en el establecimiento de Erakin, el comerciante millonario,
sentences.translationRu: но в заведении Еракина, богатого купца-миллионера,
sentences.original: but at Erakin's, the millionaire shopkeeper's,
they were trying the new electric lights,
which flickered brightly,

sentences.text: Mucha gente se había reunido alrededor para verlas.
sentences.translationRu: Вокруг собралась большая толпа, чтобы посмотреть на них.
sentences.original: and a crowd of people were gathered round.

sentences.text: Luego vinieron calles anchas, oscuras y desiertas,
sentences.translationRu: Затем шли широкие тёмные пустынные улицы,
sentences.original: Then came wide, dark, deserted streets,
one after another;
then the highroad,
the open country,
the fragrance of pines.

sentences.text: De repente el obispo vio ante él
sentences.original: And suddenly there rose up before the bishop's eyes
a white turreted wall,

sentences.text: y detrás un alto campanario a la luz plena de la luna.
sentences.translationRu: а за ней высокую колокольню в полном лунном свете.
sentences.original: and behind it a tall belfry in the full moonlight,

sentences.text: A su lado brillaban cinco cúpulas doradas.
sentences.original: and beside it five shining, golden cupolas:
this was the Pankratievsky Monastery,
in which Bishop Pyotr lived.
And here, too,
high above the monastery,

sentences.text: estaba la luna silenciosa y soñadora.
sentences.original: was the silent, dreamy moon.

sentences.text: El carruaje entró por la puerta
sentences.original: The carriage drove in at the gate,
crunching over the sand;

sentences.text: En la luz de la luna se veían figuras oscuras de monjes aquí y allá,
sentences.translationRu: В лунном свете то здесь, то там виднелись тёмные фигуры монахов,
sentences.original: here and there in the moonlight there were glimpses of dark monastic figures,

sentences.text: y se oían pasos sobre las losas.
sentences.translationRu: и слышались шаги по каменным плитам.
sentences.original: and there was the sound of footsteps on the flag-stones. . . .
"You know, your holiness,

sentences.text: que su mamá llegó mientras estaba fuera
sentences.translationRu: ваша мама приехала, пока вас не было, —
sentences.original: your mamma arrived while you were away,"

sentences.text: —le informó el hermano lego cuando el obispo entró en su celda.
sentences.translationRu: сообщил монастырский служка, когда епископ вошёл в свою келью.
sentences.original: the lay brother informed the bishop as he went into his cell.
"My mother?
When did she come?"
"Before the evening service.
She asked first where you were
and then she went to the convent."

sentences.text: —¡Entonces era ella a quien vi en la iglesia hace un momento!
sentences.translationRu: — Значит, это она, кого я видел в церкви только что!
sentences.original: "Then it was her I saw in the church, just now!
Oh, Lord!"

sentences.text: Y el obispo se rió de alegría.
sentences.original: And the bishop laughed with joy.

sentences.text: —Me encargó que dijera a su ilustrísima
sentences.original: "She bade me tell your holiness,"
the lay brother went on,
"that she would come to-morrow.
She had a little girl with her--her grandchild, I suppose.

sentences.text: Se hospedan en la posada de Ovsyannikov.
sentences.original: They are staying at Ovsyannikov's inn."
"What time is it now?"
"A little after eleven."
"Oh, how vexing!"

sentences.text: El obispo se sentó un rato en el salón,
sentences.original: The bishop sat for a little while in the parlour,
hesitating,

sentences.text: y sin poder creer que fuera tan tarde.
sentences.translationRu: и не в силах поверить, что уже так поздно.
sentences.original: and as it were refusing to believe it was so late.

sentences.text: Tenía los brazos y las piernas entumecidos
sentences.translationRu: Руки и ноги у него затекли,
sentences.original: His arms and legs were stiff,
his head ached.
He was hot
and uncomfortable.
After resting a little
he went into his bedroom,

sentences.text: y también allí se sentó un rato
sentences.original: and there, too, he sat a little,
still thinking of his mother;

sentences.text: Oía cómo el hermano lego se marchaba
sentences.original: he could hear the lay brother going away,

sentences.text: y al padre Sisoy tosiendo al otro lado de la pared.
sentences.translationRu: и как отец Сисой кашляет по ту сторону стены.
sentences.original: and Father Sisoy coughing the other side of the wall.

sentences.text: El reloj del monasterio dio el cuarto.
sentences.original: The monastery clock struck a quarter.

sentences.text: El obispo se cambió de ropa
sentences.original: The bishop changed his clothes

sentences.text: y empezó a leer las oraciones antes de dormir.
sentences.original: and began reading the prayers before sleep.
He read attentively
those old, long familiar prayers,

sentences.text: y al mismo tiempo pensaba en su madre.
sentences.translationRu: и в то же время думал о матери.
sentences.original: and at the same time thought about his mother.
She had nine children
and about forty grandchildren.

sentences.text: En otro tiempo había vivido con su marido, el diácono,
sentences.translationRu: В своё время она жила с мужем, дьяконом,
sentences.original: At one time, she had lived with her husband, the deacon,
in a poor village;
she had lived there a very long time

sentences.text: desde los diecisiete hasta los sesenta años.
sentences.original: from the age of seventeen to sixty.

sentences.text: El obispo la recordaba desde la primera infancia,
sentences.translationRu: Епископ помнил её с раннего детства,
sentences.original: The bishop remembered her from early childhood,
almost from the age of three,
and--how he had loved her!

sentences.text: ¡Qué dulce y precioso había sido aquella infancia,
sentences.translationRu: Какое сладкое и бесценное было то детство,
sentences.original: Sweet, precious childhood,
always fondly remembered!
Why did it, that long-past time
that could never return,

sentences.text: parecía más brillante, más pleno y más festivo
sentences.original: why did it seem brighter, fuller, and more festive

sentences.text: de lo que había sido en realidad?
sentences.translationRu: чем оно было на самом деле?
sentences.original: than it had really been?

sentences.text: Cuando en su niñez o su juventud estaba enfermo,
sentences.translationRu: Когда в детстве или в юности он болел,
sentences.original: When in his childhood or youth he had been ill,

sentences.text: ¡qué tierna y compasiva había sido su madre!
sentences.translationRu: какой нежной и сострадательной была его мать!
sentences.original: how tender and sympathetic his mother had been!

sentences.text: Ahora sus oraciones se mezclaban con los recuerdos,
sentences.translationRu: Теперь его молитвы смешивались с воспоминаниями,
sentences.original: And now his prayers mingled with the memories,

sentences.text: que brillaban cada vez más como una llama.
sentences.translationRu: которые разгорались всё ярче, как пламя.
sentences.original: which gleamed more and more brightly like a flame,

sentences.text: Y los recuerdos no le impedían seguir rezando.
sentences.translationRu: И воспоминания не мешали ему молиться.
sentences.original: and the prayers did not hinder his thinking of his mother.
When he had finished his prayers
he undressed
and lay down,

sentences.text: En cuanto se hizo la oscuridad,
sentences.original: and at once, as soon as it was dark,

sentences.text: ante su mente aparecieron su padre muerto, su madre
sentences.translationRu: перед его мысленным взором возникли умерший отец, мать
sentences.original: there rose before his mind his dead father, his mother,
his native village Lesopolye . . .
the creak of wheels,
the bleat of sheep,

sentences.text: las campanas de la iglesia en las brillantes mañanas de verano,
sentences.translationRu: колокола церкви в ярких летних утрах,
sentences.original: the church bells on bright summer mornings,
the gypsies under the window--

sentences.text: ¡Qué dulce era pensar en todo eso!
sentences.translationRu: Как сладко было думать обо всём этом!
sentences.original: oh, how sweet to think of it!

sentences.text: Recordó al sacerdote de Lesopolye, el padre Semión:
sentences.translationRu: Он вспомнил священника из Лесополья, отца Семёна:
sentences.original: He remembered the priest of Lesopolye, Father Simeon--mild, gentle, kindly;
he was a lean little man,

sentences.text: mientras que su hijo, estudiante de teología,
sentences.translationRu: тогда как его сын, студент духовной семинарии,
sentences.original: while his son, a divinity student,
was a huge fellow

sentences.text: que hablaba con voz retumbante de bajo.
sentences.original: and talked in a roaring bass voice.

sentences.text: Una vez el hijo del sacerdote se enfadó mucho con la cocinera
sentences.translationRu: Однажды сын священника очень рассердился на кухарку
sentences.original: The priest's son had flown into a rage with the cook
and abused her: "Ah, you Jehud's ass!"
and Father Simeon overhearing it,
said not a word,
and was only ashamed

sentences.text: porque no recordaba dónde se mencionaba ese insulto en la Biblia.
sentences.translationRu: потому что не помнил, где в Библии упоминается это слово.
sentences.original: because he could not remember where such an ass was mentioned in the Bible.
After him

sentences.translationRu: священником в Лесополье стал отец Дамиан,
sentences.original: the priest at Lesopolye had been Father Demyan,
who used to drink heavily,

sentences.text: A veces bebía tanto que veía serpientes verdes,
sentences.translationRu: Иногда он пил до того, что видел зелёных змей,
sentences.original: and at times drank till he saw green snakes,

sentences.text: y por eso le llamaban Demián el que Veía Serpientes.
sentences.translationRu: и поэтому его прозвали Дамиан Змеевидец.
sentences.original: and was even nicknamed Demyan Snakeseer.

sentences.translationRu: Школьным учителем в Лесополье был Матвей Николаич,
sentences.original: The schoolmaster at Lesopolye was Matvey Nikolaitch,
who had been a divinity student,
a kind and intelligent man,
but he, too, was a drunkard;
he never beat the schoolchildren,

sentences.text: pero en la pared de su cuarto colgaba siempre un manojo de ramas de abedul.
sentences.translationRu: но на стене его комнаты всегда висела связка берёзовых прутьев.
sentences.original: but for some reason he always had hanging on his wall a bunch of birch-twigs,

sentences.text: Debajo había una inscripción completamente sin sentido en latín:
sentences.translationRu: Под ней висела совершенно бессмысленная надпись по-латыни:
sentences.original: and below it an utterly meaningless inscription in Latin:
"Betula kinderbalsamica secuta."

sentences.text: Tenía un perro negro y peludo
sentences.translationRu: У него была лохматая чёрная собака
sentences.original: He had a shaggy black dog
whom he called Syntax.

sentences.text: Su ilustrísima se rió al recordarlo.
sentences.translationRu: Его преосвященство засмеялся, вспоминая всё это.
sentences.original: And his holiness laughed.
Six miles from Lesopolye
was the village Obnino
with a wonder-working ikon.

sentences.text: En verano llevaban el icono en procesión por los pueblos vecinos
sentences.translationRu: Летом икону носили крестным ходом по соседним сёлам
sentences.original: In the summer they used to carry the ikon in procession about the neighbouring villages

sentences.text: y tocaban las campanas todo el día.
sentences.translationRu: и звонили в колокола весь день.
sentences.original: and ring the bells the whole day long;
first in one village
and then in another,
and it used to seem to the bishop then

sentences.text: que la alegría temblaba en el aire.
sentences.original: that joy was quivering in the air,

sentences.text: Él —en aquellos tiempos se llamaba Pavlusha—
sentences.original: and he (in those days his name was Pavlusha)

sentences.text: seguía el icono descalzo y sin sombrero,
sentences.translationRu: шёл за иконой босиком и без шапки,
sentences.original: used to follow the ikon, bareheaded and barefoot,

sentences.text: con fe sencilla y una sonrisa inocente.
sentences.translationRu: с простодушной верой и наивной улыбкой.
sentences.original: with naïve faith, with a naïve smile,
infinitely happy.
In Obnino,
he remembered now,
there were always a lot of people,

sentences.text: El sacerdote de allí, el padre Alexey,
sentences.original: and the priest there, Father Alexey,

sentences.text: para ahorrar tiempo durante la misa
sentences.translationRu: чтобы сэкономить время во время службы,
sentences.original: to save time during mass,

sentences.original: used to make his deaf nephew Ilarion read

sentences.text: los nombres de las personas por las que se pedían oraciones.

sentences.original: the names of those for whose health or whose souls' peace prayers were asked.
Ilarion used to read them,

sentences.text: y a veces recibía cinco o diez kopeks por el servicio.
sentences.translationRu: и иногда получал за это пять или десять копеек.
sentences.original: now and then getting a five or ten kopeck piece for the service,

sentences.text: Ya cuando era viejo y calvo,
sentences.translationRu: Уже когда он стал старым и лысым,
sentences.original: and only when he was grey and bald,

sentences.text: cuando la vida casi había terminado para él,
sentences.translationRu: когда жизнь почти закончилась для него,
sentences.original: when life was nearly over,

sentences.text: vio escrito en uno de los papeles:
sentences.translationRu: он увидел написанное на одной из бумажек:
sentences.original: he suddenly saw written on one of the pieces of paper:
"What a fool you are, Ilarion."

sentences.text: Hasta los quince años por lo menos
sentences.translationRu: До пятнадцати лет по меньшей мере
sentences.original: Up to fifteen at least

sentences.text: Pavlusha había sido poco aplicado y perezoso en los estudios.
sentences.translationRu: Павлуша был неразвитым и ленивым в учёбе.
sentences.original: Pavlusha was undeveloped and idle at his lessons, so much so

sentences.text: Tanto que pensaron en sacarlo de la escuela clerical
sentences.translationRu: Настолько, что думали забрать его из духовного училища
sentences.original: that they thought of taking him away from the clerical school
and putting him into a shop;

sentences.text: Un día fue a la oficina de correos de Obnino
sentences.translationRu: Однажды он пошёл на почту в Обнино
sentences.original: one day, going to the post at Obnino
for letters,

sentences.text: y se quedó mirando mucho tiempo a los empleados.
sentences.original: he had stared a long time at the post-office clerks and asked:
"Allow me to ask,
how do you get your salary,
every month or every day?"

and at last drove home. Between one and two o'clock he had welcome visitors dining with him--his mother and his niece Katya, a child of eight years old. All dinner-time the spring sunshine was streaming in at the windows, throwing bright light on the white tablecloth and on Katya's red hair. Through the double windows they could hear the noise of the rooks and the notes of the starlings in the garden. "It is nine years since we have met," said the old lady. "And when I looked at you in the monastery yesterday, good Lord! you've not changed a bit, except maybe you are thinner and your beard is a little longer. Holy Mother, Queen of Heaven! Yesterday at the evening service no one could help crying. I, too, as I looked at you, suddenly began crying, though I couldn't say why. His Holy Will!" And in spite of the affectionate tone in which she said this, he could see she was constrained as though she were uncertain whether to address him formally or familiarly, to laugh or not, and that she felt herself more a deacon's widow than his mother. And Katya gazed without blinking at her uncle, his holiness, as though trying to discover what sort of a person he was. Her hair sprang up from under the comb and the velvet ribbon and stood out like a halo; she had a turned-up nose and sly eyes. The child had broken a glass before sitting down to dinner, and now her grandmother, as she talked, moved away from Katya first a wineglass and then a tumbler. The bishop listened to his mother and remembered how many, many years ago she used to take him and his brothers and sisters to relations whom she considered rich; in those days she was taken up with the care of her children, now with her grandchildren, and she had brought Katya. . . . "Your sister, Varenka, has four children," she told him; "Katya, here, is the eldest. And your brother-in-law Father Ivan fell sick, God knows of what, and died three days before the Assumption; and my poor Varenka is left a beggar." "And how is Nikanor getting on?" the bishop asked about his eldest brother. "He is all right, thank God. Though he has nothing much, yet he can live. Only there is one thing: his son, my grandson Nikolasha, did not want to go into the Church; he has gone to the university to be a doctor. He thinks it is better; but who knows! His Holy Will!" "Nikolasha cuts up dead people," said Katya, spilling water over her knees. "Sit still, child," her grandmother observed calmly, and took the glass out of her hand. "Say a prayer, and go on eating." "How long it is since we have seen each other!" said the bishop, and he tenderly stroked his mother's hand and shoulder; "and I missed you abroad, mother, I missed you dreadfully." "Thank you." "I used to sit in the evenings at the open window, lonely and alone; often there was music playing, and all at once I used to be overcome with homesickness and felt as though I would give everything only to be at home and see you." His mother smiled, beamed, but at once she made a grave face and said: "Thank you." His mood suddenly changed. He looked at his mother and could not understand how she had come by that respectfulness, that timid expression of face: what was it for?

sentences.translationRu: что он стал тоньше, слабее и ничтожнее всех.
sentences.original: that he was thinner, weaker, and more insignificant than anyone,

sentences.translationRu: И в тот момент она забыла,
sentences.original: and now she forgot
that he was a bishop,

sentences.text: Lo besó como a un niño muy querido y muy cercano.
sentences.translationRu: Она целовала его как очень любимого и очень близкого ребёнка.
sentences.original: and kissed him as though he were a child very near and very dear to her.
"Pavlusha, darling," she said; "my own, my darling son! . . .
Why are you like this?
Pavlusha, answer me!"
Katya, pale and severe,

sentences.text: estaba de pie a su lado.
sentences.original: stood beside her,

sentences.text: No entendía qué le pasaba a su tío
sentences.translationRu: Она не понимала, что происходит с дядей
sentences.original: unable to understand what was the matter with her uncle,

sentences.text: ni por qué había tanto sufrimiento en el rostro de su abuela.
sentences.translationRu: и почему на лице бабушки столько страдания.
sentences.original: why there was such a look of suffering on her grandmother's face,

sentences.text: No entendía por qué la abuela decía palabras tan tristes y conmovedoras.
sentences.translationRu: Не понимала, почему бабушка говорит такие грустные и трогательные слова.
sentences.original: why she was saying such sad and touching things.

sentences.text: Ya el obispo no podía pronunciar ni una palabra
sentences.translationRu: Сам епископ уже не мог произнести ни слова
sentences.original: By now he could not utter a word,
he could understand nothing,

sentences.text: Se imaginaba que era un hombre sencillo y corriente.
sentences.translationRu: Ему казалось, что он простой обычный человек.
sentences.original: and he imagined he was a simple ordinary man,

sentences.text: Se imaginaba que caminaba rápido y con alegría por el campo,
sentences.translationRu: Казалось, он идёт быстро и радостно по полю,
sentences.original: that he was walking quickly, cheerfully through the fields,

sentences.text: golpeando el suelo con su bastón.
sentences.original: tapping with his stick,
while above him was the open sky
bathed in sunshine,

sentences.text: Y él era libre como un pájaro
sentences.translationRu: И он был свободен, как птица,
sentences.original: and that he was free now as a bird

sentences.text: y podía ir a donde quisiera.
sentences.original: and could go where he liked!
"Pavlusha, my darling son, answer me,"
the old woman was saying.
"What is it?
My own!"
"Don't disturb his holiness,"
Sisoy said angrily,
walking about the room.
"Let him sleep . . .
what's the use . . .
it's no good. . . ."
Three doctors arrived,
consulted together,
and went away again.
The day was long,
incredibly long,
then the night came on
and passed slowly, slowly,
and towards morning on Saturday

sentences.text: el hermano lego entró en la sala
sentences.original: the lay brother went in

sentences.text: donde la madre anciana estaba tumbada en el sofá.
sentences.translationRu: где старая мать лежала на диване.
sentences.original: to the old mother who was lying on the sofa in the parlour,

sentences.text: Le pidió que fuera al dormitorio:
sentences.translationRu: Он попросил её пройти в спальню:
sentences.original: and asked her to go into the bedroom:
the bishop had just breathed his last.

sentences.text: Al día siguiente era el Domingo de Pascua.
sentences.translationRu: На следующий день было Пасхальное воскресенье.
sentences.original: Next day was Easter Sunday.

sentences.text: En la ciudad había cuarenta y dos iglesias
sentences.translationRu: В городе было сорок две церкви
sentences.original: There were forty-two churches
and six monasteries in the town;

sentences.text: El sonoro y alegre repique de campanas
sentences.original: the sonorous, joyful clang of the bells

sentences.text: sonó sobre la ciudad desde la mañana hasta la noche sin parar.
sentences.translationRu: звучал над городом с утра до ночи без остановки.
sentences.original: hung over the town from morning till night unceasingly,
setting the spring air aquiver;
the birds were singing,

sentences.text: y el sol brillaba con fuerza.
sentences.original: the sun was shining brightly.

sentences.text: La gran plaza del mercado estaba llena de ruido:
sentences.translationRu: На большой рыночной площади царил шум:
sentences.original: The big market square was noisy,
swings were going,
barrel organs were playing,
accordions were squeaking,

sentences.text: Se escuchaban voces borrachas y a gritos.
sentences.original: drunken voices were shouting.
After midday

sentences.text: la gente empezó a pasear por la calle principal.
sentences.translationRu: люди начали прогуливаться по главной улице.
sentences.original: people began driving up and down the principal street.
In short,
all was merriment,
everything was satisfactory,

sentences.translationRu: точно так же, как в прошлом году.
sentences.original: just as it had been the year before,

sentences.text: Y probablemente así sería también el año siguiente.
sentences.translationRu: И, скорее всего, так же будет и в следующем году.
sentences.original: and as it will be in all likelihood next year.
A month later

sentences.text: nombraron a un nuevo obispo sufragáneo.
sentences.original: a new suffragan bishop was appointed,

sentences.text: Y nadie pensó más en el obispo Pyotr.
sentences.translationRu: И никто больше не думал об епископе Пётре.
sentences.original: and no one thought anything more of Bishop Pyotr,

sentences.text: Con el tiempo fue completamente olvidado.
sentences.original: and afterwards he was completely forgotten.
And only the dead man's old mother,

sentences.text: que vive hoy con su yerno el diácono en una pequeña ciudad remota,
sentences.translationRu: которая живёт сейчас у своего зятя-дьякона в маленьком далёком городе,
sentences.original: who is living to-day with her son-in-law the deacon in a remote little district town,

sentences.text: Cuando sale por las noches a buscar a su vaca
sentences.translationRu: Когда она выходит по ночам пригнать корову
sentences.original: when she goes out at night to bring her cow in

sentences.text: y encuentra a otras mujeres en el pasto,
sentences.translationRu: и встречает других женщин на пастбище,
sentences.original: and meets other women at the pasture,

sentences.text: empieza a hablar de sus hijos y sus nietos.
sentences.translationRu: она начинает рассказывать о своих детях и внуках.
sentences.original: begins talking of her children and her grandchildren,

sentences.text: Y dice que tuvo un hijo que fue obispo.
sentences.translationRu: И говорит, что у неё был сын, который стал епископом.
sentences.original: and says that she had a son a bishop,
and this she says timidly,

sentences.text: con miedo de que no la crean.
sentences.original: afraid that she may not be believed. . . .
And, indeed, there are some

sentences.translationRu: есть те, кто не верит ей.
sentences.original: who do not believe her .
